# Tableau Dashboard Build Guide
## HR Employee Attrition Dashboard

**Dataset file:** `hr_attrition_clean.csv` (generated by running `hr_attrition_prep.ipynb`)
**Tool:** Tableau Public (free — download at https://public.tableau.com/en-us/s/download)

---

## Step 1: Connect Your Data
1. Open Tableau Public
2. On the start screen, click **"Text File"** under Connect
3. Navigate to this folder and select `hr_attrition_clean.csv`
4. Verify data loaded correctly — you should see 1,470 rows in the Data Source tab
5. Click **"Sheet 1"** at the bottom to start building

---

## Step 2: Sheet 1 — Overall Attrition KPI
1. Rename the sheet: double-click tab → type "KPI - Attrition Rate"
2. Drag `Attrition Binary` to the Text card on the Marks shelf
3. Change aggregation: right-click → Measure → Average
4. Click the Text card → Format as Percentage (2 decimal places)
5. This single number = your headline attrition rate

---

## Step 3: Sheet 2 — Attrition by Department (Bar Chart)
1. New sheet → rename "Attrition by Department"
2. Drag `Department` to **Columns**
3. Drag `Attrition Binary` to **Rows** → set to AVG → format as %
4. On the Marks shelf, change mark type to **Bar**
5. Drag `Attrition Binary` (AVG) to **Color** — use Red-Blue diverging palette
6. Drag `Attrition Binary` (AVG, formatted %) to **Label**
7. Sort bars: click the sort descending icon on the axis

---

## Step 4: Sheet 3 — Attrition by Age Group (Line Chart)
1. New sheet → rename "Attrition by Age Group"
2. Drag `Age Group` to **Columns**
3. Drag `Attrition Binary` (AVG) to **Rows** → format as %
4. Change mark type to **Line**
5. Add circle markers: on the Marks shelf, click **"Path"** → select Circle
6. Add labels at each point

---

## Step 5: Sheet 4 — Income vs. Attrition (Box Plot)
1. New sheet → rename "Income vs Attrition"
2. Drag `Attrition` to **Columns**
3. Drag `Monthly Income` to **Rows**
4. Change mark type to **Circle** first, then hold Ctrl and drag `Monthly Income` again to Rows
5. Right-click the axis → Add Reference Line → Box Plot
6. Color: filter on Attrition → Yes = Red, No = Blue

---

## Step 6: Sheet 5 — Overtime Impact (Bar Chart)
1. New sheet → rename "Overtime Impact"
2. Drag `Over Time` to **Columns**
3. Drag `Attrition Binary` (AVG %) to **Rows**
4. Color the "Yes" bar: click the bar → Format → use Red
5. Add labels showing the %

---

## Step 7: Sheet 6 — Attrition Heatmap
1. New sheet → rename "Dept × Role Heatmap"
2. Drag `Department` to **Columns**, `Job Role` to **Rows**
3. Drag `Attrition Binary` (AVG) to **Color** (Red-Blue diverging)
4. Drag `Attrition Binary` (AVG, formatted %) to **Label**
5. Change mark type to **Square**

---

## Step 8: Build the Dashboard
1. Click **"New Dashboard"** at the bottom
2. Set size: click **"Device Preview"** → select **Fixed Size** → 1200 × 900
3. Arrange your sheets:
   ```
   [ KPI Card ] [ KPI Card ] [ KPI Card ]
   [ Attrition by Department    ] [ Age Line Chart ]
   [ Heatmap                    ] [ Overtime Bars  ]
   ```
4. Add a title text object at the top: **"HR Employee Attrition Dashboard"**
5. Add a filter: drag `Department` to the dashboard canvas → Show as dropdown

---

## Step 9: Polish & Publish
1. Ensure consistent color scheme (Red = attrition risk, Blue = retained)
2. Add a text box with your name and date
3. Go to **File → Save to Tableau Public**
4. Log in with your free Tableau Public account
5. Name your workbook: `HR Attrition Analysis - Shantanu Deshmukh`
6. After publishing, **copy the URL** — this goes on your resume under Project 2

---

## Key Findings Your Dashboard Should Show
- Overall attrition rate ~16%
- Sales department has higher attrition than R&D
- Employees aged 18–25 have the highest attrition rate
- Overtime employees churn at nearly 2x the rate of non-overtime workers
- Lower income employees (Job Level 1) leave at higher rates

---
*Dashboard project by Shantanu Deshmukh | MS Computer Science, NJIT*
